channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Introducing okta-skill: Zero-Config Okta SSO Auto-Login for AI Coding Assistants
item.description: Tired of manually logging into internal tools protected by Okta SSO? okta-skill brings seamless authentication to your AI coding assistant workflow. 🎯 What is okta-skill? okta-skill is an agent...

item.title: Prometheus ve Grafana'yı Derinlemesine Anlamak — TSDB, PromQL ve Custom Exporter
item.description: Neden TSDB? Normal bir veritabanında bir satır güncellenir — eski değer gider, yeni değer gelir. Monitoring'de bu işe yaramaz. "CPU şu an %45" bilgisi değil, "CPU son 1 saatte nasıl değişti"...

item.title: Virtual keys per tenant: ditching our custom LLM billing layer
item.description: TL;DR: We had 11,247 lines of Python middleware handling per-tenant LLM cost attribution, rate limiting, and provider failover. Replaced about 60% of it with Bifrost's virtual keys and governance...

item.title: Database Partitioning: Does It Always Provide Scalability?
item.description: Partitioning: The Magic Wand of Scalability? When database partitioning comes to mind, "scalability" is usually the first thing that pops up. The idea is that dividing large tables into smaller,...

item.title: Why your quantized LLM loses its MTP heads and how to keep them
item.description: The frustrating problem Last month a teammate pinged me with a classic head-scratcher. He'd taken a base model with multi-token prediction (MTP) heads, ran it through a standard quantization...

item.title: AI coding startup Cognition raises $1B at $25B pre-money valuation
item.description: As Cognition reaches $492 in annualized revenue run rate, it more than doubled its valuation in eight months, it says.

item.title: Sony’s first RGB TV is a statement piece
item.description: The first wave of RGB LED TVs are fighting for their spot in the TV hierarchy. They need to outperform OLED TVs in brightness and color (because they'll never match OLED's contrast), and they need...

item.title: AI Writes Code Faster. Why Hasn’t Delivery?
item.description: The bottleneck didn’t disappear — it moved downstream. When coding gets 10× faster but review, CI, and release don’t, you don’t get 10× delivery. You get a longer queue behind the keyboard. If...

item.title: Shopify vs Shopify Plus (2026): The Real Difference No One Talks About
item.description: I spent three weeks helping a brand migrate from Shopify Advanced to Shopify Plus. Halfway through, I realized most of the blog posts comparing these two plans were either outdated or written by...

item.title: Advent of Code 2015 days 3-8 in Clojure
item.description: Yeah, I'm starting to think my initial comment about taking until the heat death of the universe to complete this wasn't far off the mark. I'm not really slow at finishing the tasks, it's just...

item.title: How I Built an Open-Source Social Media Scheduler with Laravel
item.description: I wanted to get consistent at posting: one calendar across every network, planned a week ahead instead of thrown together the morning of. The problem was that every scheduler I tried wanted my...

item.title: AI tried to bury this politician — now people have actually heard of him
item.description: By the time that the Democratic primary for New York's 12th congressional district wraps up in June, Anthropic and OpenAI will have spent millions on their battle over the political future of AI:...

item.title: Mastering Dependency Injection in .NET: A Software Engineer's Secret Weapon
item.description: Imagine you are building a modern car. Instead of welding the engine directly to the chassis, you bolt it in. Why? Because if the engine breaks—or if you want to upgrade from a gas engine to an...

item.title: Here’s how Google is responding to Fitbit users who don’t like the new Health app
item.description: After a flood of complaints about the Google Health app that just replaced Fitbit, Google has responded with a list of changes that will roll out starting this week. Google is addressing some of...

item.title: Sony’s DualSense controllers are almost 30 percent off
item.description: Sony has a tradition of marking down its PlayStation 5 hardware a couple of times a year, and one of those opportunities to save is here through June 10th. Its latest Days of Play sale is...

item.title: Robinhood will let your AI agent trade stocks and make (or lose) lots of money
item.description: Robinhood is opening its trading platform to AI agents. In an announcement on Wednesday, Robinhood says traders can now create a separate account for an AI agent and add a specific amount of...

item.title: Startup Battlefield 200 applications close today: Nominate a founder or submit your startup
item.description: Today is the final day to apply or nominate a startup for Startup Battlefield 200. Once the clock strikes 11:59 p.m. PT, the window closes on your chance to compete for $100,000 in equity-free...

item.title: ElevenLabs’s new music generation model can switch genres mid-track
item.description: ElevenLabs' new model will let users regenerate a section of a song without affecting rest of the track

item.title: FBI’s 2025 Internet Crime Report
item.description: The 2025 Internet Crime Report was published a few weeks ago, but I only just saw it. Lots of interesting statistics. Press release. News articles.

item.title: SOND, a sleep tech startup from Bose’s former head of sleep, exits stealth with $7M
item.description: SOND, a startup led by Bose’s former head of sleep products, emerged from stealth with $7M in funding for its AI-powered sleep earbuds.

item.title: TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 Early Bird ticket savings end in 3 days
item.description: There are only 3 days left to save up to $410 on your ticket to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026. Early Bird pricing ends May 29 at 11:59 p.m. PT, and once the deadline passes, ticket prices increase. If...

item.title: Former Google and Apple Researchers Launch a Startup to Build AI’s Missing Feedback Loop
item.description: Trajectory is betting the rapid iteration cycle that supercharged vibe-coding can help all kinds of companies build AI products that learn continuously.

item.title: This smart bird feeder captures more of my backyard drama
item.description: Since moving to South Carolina's Lowcountry, I've been spellbound by the myriad of beautiful birds that share the coast with us - ospreys raising their babies in towering nests beside the road to...

item.title: This Ferrari should have been a Volkswagen
item.description: It may be the NBA playoffs but right now Ferrari is getting dunked on more than anyone. Whether they own a Ferrari or just have a poster of a Testarossa on their wall, fans in the Ferrari-verse...

item.title: China is increasingly keeping its best AI talent to itself
item.description: China's AI boom is producing world-class talent, and Beijing is increasingly reluctant to let them go elsewhere.

item.title: 007 First Light is like a James Bond movie in the best way possible
item.description: The James Bond franchise is nothing if not a spectacle: Aside from the explosions and gunfights, even its calmer moments are dripping in the pomp and glamour of elite ostentation and luxury...

item.title: 5 Steps to Managing Shadow AI Tools Without Slowing Down Employees
item.description: When an employee installs an AI writing assistant, connects a coding copilot to their IDE, or starts summarizing meetings with a new browser tool, they are doing exactly what a productive employee...

item.title: ClickHouse triples anualized revenue to $250M, charting a path toward an IPO
item.description: The database provider is eyeing a public debut within the next few years.

item.title: YouTube will now automatically label AI videos
item.description: YouTube will now automatically label videos that use significant photorealistic AI, instead of relying solely on creators to disclose AI-generated content themselves. It's also making AI labels...

item.title: Win cool gadgets we can’t keep because The Verge has ethics
item.description: When people learn I work for The Verge, they inevitably ask: “Do you get to keep the gadgets?” Now that I film Today I’m Toying With, a video series where I share the joy of tech, I get that...
